    }}>{`Building your first UXP plugin`}</h1>
    <p>{`This tutorial will walk you through building your very first UXP plugin. You will learn to scaffold one from an existing template, customize it, and run it in Premiere.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "prerequisites"

          "href": "https://www.cursor.com/"
        }}>{`Cursor`}</a>{`.`}</li>

    <p>{`If you have closed the plugin, you can always show it again from Premiere's `}<strong parentName="p">{`Window`}</strong>{` > `}<strong parentName="p">{`UXP Plugins`}</strong>{` menu.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "3-edit-the-plugins-ui"
    }}>{`3. Edit the plugin's UI`}</h3>
    <p>{`Now that your plugin is running in Premiere, let's make it your own. Open the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`index.html`}</inlineCode>{` file in your code editor. This file contains the HTML code that defines the visual content of your plugin's panel.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
  <head>
    <script src="main.js"></script>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css" />
  </head>
  <body>
    <h4>Application Info</h4>
    <div class="main-div">
      <sp-body id="plugin-body"> </sp-body>
    </div>
    <footer>
      <sp-button id="btnPopulate">Populate Application Info</sp-button>
      <sp-button id="clear-btn">Clear Application Info</sp-button>
    </footer>
  </body>
</html>
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Let's change the heading to something slightly different:`}</p>

    <InlineAlert slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Manifest changes`}</p>
    <p>{`If you make any changes to the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` file, you must manually unload and reload the plugin. In the UXP Developer Tool, in the row for your plugin, click `}<strong parentName="p">{`Unload`}</strong>{` and then `}<strong parentName="p">{`Load & Watch`}</strong>{` once more.`}</p>
    <p>{`Invisible plugins`}</p>
    <p>{`By setting `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"hideFromMenu"`}</inlineCode>{` to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`true`}</inlineCode>{` (in your plugin manifest's `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`hostUIContext`}</inlineCode>{` section), you can create a UXP plugin that will get called at application launch (and so can register for other messages), but which will `}<em parentName="p">{`not`}</em>{` appear in the UXP Plugins menu.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "4-test-the-plugins-logic"
    }}>{`4. Test the plugin's logic`}</h3>
    <p>{`Now let's check what this UXP plugin can do. Open a sample project in Premiere; if you don't have one, go to the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Window`}</strong>{` > `}<strong parentName="p">{`Learn`}</strong>{` menu and download a sample from the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Tutorials`}</strong>{` section.`}</p>
    <p>{`Now click the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Populate Application Info`}</strong>{` button in the plugin's panel.`}</p>

    <p>{`The plugin will fetch the active project and sequence information and display it in the panel. The implementation lives in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`index.js`}</inlineCode>{` file, and it uses the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../ppro_reference/index.md"
      }}>{`Premiere APIs`}</a>{` to fetch the information. You can open the file and explore the code to see how it works.`}</p>
